name: Stencil (Web Component Compiler)

performance.notes: Compiled to optimized Web Components; lazy loading

agent_readiness.notes: TypeScript compiler for Web Components with JSX-like syntax. @Component({tag: 'my-button', styleUrl: 'button.css'}). @Prop() for inputs. @State() for internal state. JSX render(). @Watch() for reactive changes. @Event() for custom events. Outputs: dist (Web Components), dist-custom-elements, www (standalone). Auto-generates React/Vue/Angular wrappers. Used by Ionic Framework. Compiles to tiny optimized native Web Components. Better tooling than Lit for large teams.

gotchas: JSX syntax despite compiling to Web Components — requires compiler step
Generated framework wrappers may lag behind core
Heavier toolchain than Lit (webpack/rollup build required)
best_when: Enterprise design system with React/Vue/Angular framework wrappers needed
Ionic-based mobile/web hybrid app component library
Large teams wanting TypeScript-first component authoring
When framework wrapper generation from one codebase is required
avoid_when: Small teams (Lit simpler for basic Web Components)
Pure browser Web Component projects without build tools
When React-only components (no need for web standard)
summary: Stencil compiles TypeScript+JSX into optimized Web Components. @Component, @Prop, @State, @Event decorators. Auto-generates React/Vue/Angular framework wrappers from one codebase. Used by Ionic Framework for cross-platform components. Heavier toolchain than Lit but better for enterprise design systems needing multi-framework wrapper generation. TypeScript-first with JSX authoring.
